programmes.show_description: You'll Catch Your Death: Steed and Tara investigate a Nursing Academy who are reportedly developing a fatal sneezing epidemic.
Life with Father: Henry's wife writes to him that, given the circumstances of war, she'd understand if he found another woman. He reads between the lines that she is having an affair.
Alcoholics Unanimous: As commanding officer, Frank dismantles the Swamp's still and declares Prohibition at the 4077th. Hawkeye and Trapper get irritable and wonder if they're alcoholics.
There is Nothing Like a Nurse: The threat of an enemy parachute drop forces the 4077th to evacuate the nurses to another unit. The camp realises just how much the nurses mean to the unit.
High Noon: Matthew is challenged to a fight when it is discovered that he has been involved with a local boxing champion's girlfriend.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Back To Oakland: Mark briefly re-joins his old precinct and is paired with an old friend whom he discovers is a racist.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
We Have Forever: Part 1: Jonathan's widow dies but when he learns from God he's still needed on Earth despite her death, he rebels against him in retaliation and is stripped of his powers.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Time in a Bottle: An ambitious district attorney clashes with an alcoholic lawyer. However, he agrees to defend a fellow denizen charged with stealing a cheap bottle of wine.
Back To Oakland: Mark briefly re-joins his old precinct and is paired with an old friend whom he discovers is a racist.
...Prairie: Pilot: Feature-length pilot based on the true story of the Ingalls family and their decision to move west in search of a better life. Stars Michael Landon and Melissa Gilbert. (1974)

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: A Harvest of Friends: After moving his family from Wisconsin to Minnesota, pioneer Charles Ingalls settles on farmland by Plum Creek.
The Vigil: Nursing student Mary Ellen is devastated when she misdiagnoses Grandma's illness.
The Comeback: Having lost his scholarship, Jason accepts a job playing music at a local roadhouse to pay for his tuition.
He Healeth the Sick: Father Aidan, Ballykissangel's new priest, has a spectacularly successful first day, which proves to be a headache for Father Mac.
The Miracle of Ormston: A desperate mother tries to help her son when she hears of a statue in the village with magical healing powers. Elsewhere Jean is taking etiquette lessons.
Home Truths: Dr Napier is intrigued by an ex-colonial couple recently returned from India who settle in Tannochbrae and refuse to have contact with their daughter.
Rumpole and the Younger Generation: Rumpole encounters his first professional interaction with the Timson family.
Adam's Rib: Sick of eating liver and fish for the 11th straight day, Hawkeye hatches a plan to order forty pounds of spare ribs from his favourite restaurant, Adam's Ribs, in Chicago.
A Full Rich Day: Hawkeye records a letter to his Dad, detailing the exploits of a Turkish soldier, the loss of a Luxembourg Lieutenant's corpse and tactics to get medical care for a comrade.
Mad Dogs and Servicemen: Hawkeye notices a dog bite on the back of Radar's hand and advises him to find the dog so they can test it for rabies. Hawkeye treats a young shell-shocked GI.
...the Likely Lads?: Moving On: Appalled by the changes in Newcastle, Terry considers leaving to get a job in Berwick. Meanwhile, his friend's wanderlust sets Bob thinking.
...the Likely Lads?: I'll Never Forget Whatshername: Terry tries to track down some of his old flames, but probing into the past produces embarrassing results.
...Likely Lads?: Birthday Boy: Terry feels depressed when everyone forgets his birthday. To make up for it, Bob throws a surprise party, but preparations don't go according to plan.
Faces: Gambit and Purdey go undercover after Steed's chance encounter with an old friend. Are high-profile people being replaced by lookalikes?
All Done with Mirrors: Steed is under house arrest on suspicion of leaking secrets from the Carmadoc research institute.
Adam's Rib: Sick of eating liver and fish for the 11th straight day, Hawkeye hatches a plan to order forty pounds of spare ribs from his favourite restaurant, Adam's Ribs, in Chicago.
A Full Rich Day: Hawkeye records a letter to his Dad, detailing the exploits of a Turkish soldier, the loss of a Luxembourg Lieutenant's corpse and tactics to get medical care for a comrade.
Mad Dogs and Servicemen: Hawkeye notices a dog bite on the back of Radar's hand and advises him to find the dog so they can test it for rabies. Hawkeye treats a young shell-shocked GI.
Leaving: Matthew is set to leave home for college, and Henry is far from sad, as he's been looking forward to this day for some time.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
We Have Forever: Part 2: Jonathan, now a mortal, turns his back on everything and everyone. A young woman offers him shelter and helps him to recover his lost faith and hope.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Correspondent: A war correspondent is captured and faces execution by revolutionaries. Jonathan and Mark show him what he's missed during his life by putting work ahead of family.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Country Girls: Mean Nellie Oleson snubs Laura and Mary for wearing homespun dresses on their first day at school, and Caroline reminds Laura to be kind to Nellie to win her friendship.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Hundred 100 Mile Walk: When their wheat crop is ravaged by a hailstorm, the Walnut Grove men leave town to search for work. Charles hears about a well-paid but dangerous job.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Mr Edwards' Homecoming: Charles meets up with Isaiah Edwards, a man the Ingalls befriended on their journey to Minnesota. Back at Walnut Grove Laura is ill in bed, which concerns Mr Edwards.
The Baptism: John Walton's peace of mind is threatened when an internationally known evangelist comes to Walton's Mountain to preach and lead a baptismal service.
The Firestorm: John-Boy becomes the object of public indignation when he insists on printing excerpts from Hitler's Mein Kampf.
Bread and Water: Brendan is appalled by Siobhan's plans for a water birth. Father Aidan and his sister settle in the town but find themselves plagued by money worries.
The Magnificent Colin: The Magnificent Colin, a fortune teller, causes a stir in the village when he arrives in Ormston spreading happiness.
Days of Grace: A baby girl is found abandoned on the doorstep of Arden House. Dr Napier thinks she knows the identity of the mother.
Rumpole and the Alternative Society: Rumpole defends a West Country schoolteacher who is accused of selling drugs to a police informant.
Private Charles Lamb: When the 4077th is invited to share an Easter meal with a Greek unit, tender-hearted Radar comes up with a creative way to save the main course.
Bombed: Radar reaches HQ, who concede that they are being bombed, but not by the enemy. It's their own artillery. A patient comes into the OR with a grenade wired to his body.
Bulletin Board: Trapper writes to his daughter about life at MASH; smelly tent, smelly doctors. The bulletin board says Henry is going to give a lecture on sex.

Award-winning black comedy with Donald Sutherland and Elliott Gould. In the midst of the Korean War, the staff of a field hospital use humour and hijinks to maintain their spirits - and sanity. (1970)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Award-winning black comedy with Donald Sutherland and Elliott Gould. In the midst of the Korean War, the staff of a field hospital use humour and hijinks to maintain their spirits - and sanity. (1970)
Gnaws: The team face the horrifying prospect of confronting a giant man-eating creature living in the sewer system. Can they discover what it is and how it was created?
